schema.name: Taypro Tracker Solar Cleaning Robots
schema.description: GLYDE-X and NYUMA-X automatic solar panel cleaning robots for single-axis tracker installations in India, with CRADYL row-transfer docking.

hero.title: Solar cleaning robot for single-axis trackers — built for Indian utility scale
hero.subtitle: Tracker adoption is accelerating across Gujarat, Rajasthan, and Karnataka — but manual brush crews cannot keep pace with row length and stow angles. Taypro GLYDE-X and NYUMA-X traverse tracker tables with CRADYL row-transfer docking for 50–300 MW fleets.

cards.heading: Why tracker plants need a different cleaning robot
cards.subheading: Fixed-tilt robots and manual crews hit limits as tracker row length and MW scale grow.

card0.title: Row length & transfer
card0.body: Single-axis rows exceed 100 modules. Robots need autonomous row-end transfer — CRADYL docking stations move fleets between rows without manual carry.

card1.title: Stow angle compatibility
card1.body: Cleaning must work across tracker stow positions and seasonal tilt ranges without damaging modules or drive systems.

card2.title: Night-cycle throughput
card2.body: 100 MW tracker sites need 3–10 dry cycles per month. Manual crews cannot cover full block geometry in available cleaning windows.

card3.title: Fleet density economics
card3.body: Robots-per-MW planning must match row count, transfer points, and battery swap strategy — not generic fixed-tilt assumptions.

card4.title: Tracker vendor compatibility
card4.body: GLYDE-X and NYUMA-X are deployed across major tracker brands in India. Site assessment maps stow angles, row length, and ground clearance before fleet sizing.

card5.title: Night-window throughput modelling
card5.body: Engineering models available cleaning hours vs row count to hit 3–10 monthly cycles — the minimum for high-soiling tracker belts.

products.heading: Taypro robots for single-axis tracker arrays

product0.title: GLYDE-X — dual-pass tracker robot
product0.body: Flagship dual-pass microfiber cleaning for single-axis trackers. Highest cleaning quality per pass for premium PR recovery on utility fleets.

product1.title: NYUMA-X — PBT single-pass tracker robot
product1.body: Polymer brush technology single-pass robot for tracker sites prioritising cycle speed and capex efficiency.

product2.title: CRADYL — row transfer docking
product2.body: Autonomous docking station for robot transfer between tracker rows — essential for multi-row blocks without operator carry.

prose.heading: Tracker cleaning robots deployed at 50–300 MW in India
prose.p1: Taypro has commissioned tracker-compatible cleaning fleets on SECI and state discom projects across Gujarat and Rajasthan — including 100 MW+ single-site references. Deployment includes site mapping, CRADYL placement planning, operator training, and NECTYR onboarding.
prose.p2: Choosing GLYDE-X vs NYUMA-X depends on your PR recovery targets, dust chemistry, and capex vs cycle-speed trade-offs. Use our head-to-head comparison or request Taypro engineering to model robots-per-MW for your row layout.
prose.p3: Tracker plants also qualify for Taypro OPEX — pay per panel cleaned without upfront robot CAPEX.

projects.heading: Tracker cleaning robot project references
projects.subheading: Automatic robotic cleaning on tracker and utility-scale plants in Gujarat and India.

faq.heading: Tracker solar cleaning robot — FAQ
faq.subheading: Technical and procurement questions for single-axis tracker plants.
faq.q0: Which Taypro robot works on single-axis trackers?
faq.a0: GLYDE-X (dual-pass microfiber) and NYUMA-X (PBT single-pass) are engineered for single-axis tracker arrays. Fixed-tilt GLYDE and NYUMA are not recommended for tracker installations.
faq.q1: What is CRADYL and why do tracker sites need it?
faq.a1: CRADYL is Taypro's row-transfer docking station. It autonomously moves cleaning robots between tracker rows at row ends — eliminating manual carry on long single-axis tables.
faq.q2: How many robots per MW on a tracker plant?
faq.a2: Robots-per-MW depends on row count, row length, transfer point layout, and target cycles per month. Taypro engineers model fleet size during site assessment — typically different from fixed-tilt ratios.
faq.q3: GLYDE-X vs NYUMA-X for trackers — which should I choose?
faq.a3: GLYDE-X delivers higher cleaning quality per pass (dual-pass microfiber). NYUMA-X prioritises faster single-pass coverage and capex efficiency. Compare specifications on our head-to-head page or request Taypro engineering input.
faq.q4: Can manual brush cleaning work on tracker arrays at 50 MW+?
faq.a4: Manual crews struggle with tracker row length, night-window throughput, and consistent coverage at utility scale. Robotic dry cleaning is the economical path for 3–10 cycles per month on 50 MW+ tracker fleets.
faq.q5: Does Taypro offer OPEX on tracker plants?
faq.a5: Yes. Taypro OPEX deploys GLYDE-X or NYUMA-X with operators and bills per panel cleaned. Tracker installations carry an OPEX rate multiplier versus fixed tilt.
faq.q6: What is the commissioning timeline for tracker robots?
faq.a6: Typical mobilisation includes site mapping, CRADYL placement, connectivity setup, operator training, and NECTYR onboarding. Timeline depends on plant size and row geometry — Taypro provides a schedule during proposal.
faq.q7: How do I request a tracker site assessment?
faq.a7: Share plant MW, tracker vendor, row geometry, location, and current cleaning method. Taypro will recommend robot model, fleet size, and CAPEX or OPEX commercial terms.
faq.q8: Which tracker brands has Taypro deployed on?
faq.a8: Taypro tracker robots operate across multiple single-axis tracker installations in Gujarat and Rajasthan. Share your tracker vendor during assessment for compatibility confirmation.
faq.q9: Does robotic cleaning damage tracker drive systems?
faq.a9: Robots traverse module surfaces without loading tracker mechanical drives. Cleaning schedules respect stow positions defined in site SOPs.
faq.q10: What OPEX multiplier applies to tracker plants?
faq.a10: Tracker installations use approximately 1.8× the fixed-tilt per-panel rate due to geometry and transfer complexity. See OPEX pricing guide.
faq.q11: Can robots clean during tracker stow?
faq.a11: Cleaning programmes are scheduled for agreed stow angles and night windows — defined in the plant-specific cleaning SOP during mobilisation.

steps.heading: Commissioning tracker cleaning robots on your plant
steps.step0Body: Share MW, tracker type, row count, module dimensions, stow range, and current cleaning method. Taypro models robots-per-MW.
steps.step1Body: Dual-pass quality (GLYDE-X) vs single-pass speed and capex (NYUMA-X). Head-to-head comparison available or engineering recommendation.
steps.step2Body: Row-end docking stations positioned for autonomous transfer — minimising operator carry on 100+ module rows.
steps.step3Body: Robots mapped to blocks, operators trained on tracker-specific SOPs, connectivity commissioned.
steps.step4Body: 3–10 dry cycles monthly with NECTYR coverage logs correlated against block PR trends.

highlight.heading: Manual brush cleaning breaks at tracker scale.
highlight.lead: 100-module rows need autonomous transfer — not crews carrying brushes at row ends.
highlight.body: Taypro GLYDE-X and NYUMA-X with CRADYL row-transfer docking are engineered for Indian single-axis tracker plants from 50 MW to 300 MW.

proseSecondary.heading: Choosing a tracker-compatible cleaning robot for your PR targets
proseSecondary.p1: GLYDE-X uses Taypro's patented dual-pass microfiber system — highest dust removal per pass, ideal when soiling chemistry is sticky or cementitious and PR recovery per cycle is the priority.
proseSecondary.p2: NYUMA-X uses PBT single-pass brush technology — faster coverage per night window and lower capex, suited when dust is predominantly dry and cycle throughput is the bottleneck.
proseSecondary.p3: Both integrate with CRADYL for row transfer and NECTYR for fleet scheduling. Request engineering modelling for your tracker layout.
